analysis

Supress messages in this document so that the output is cleaner.

Import packages.

rm(list = ls())
options(warn = -1)
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(ggplot2)
library(aida)
library(BayesFactor)
library(pwr)
library(brms)
library(emmeans)
library(effsize)
library(HDInterval)

Set up theme for ggplot.

theme_set(theme_aida())

##################################################
## CSP-colors
##################################################
CSP_colors = c(
  "#7581B3", "#99C2C2", "#C65353", "#E2BA78", "#5C7457", "#575463",
  "#B0B7D4", "#66A3A3", "#DB9494", "#D49735", "#9BB096", "#D4D3D9",
  "#414C76", "#993333"
  )
# setting theme colors globally
scale_colour_discrete <- function(...) {
  scale_colour_manual(..., values = CSP_colors)
}
scale_fill_discrete <- function(...) {
  scale_fill_manual(..., values = CSP_colors)
}

Set up plots dir.

plots_dir <- "./plots"

Data Import & Preprocessing

Import data.

data <- read.csv("../../01-dataset/00-slider-data-replication-raw.csv")

Show basic information.

#show how many pps
sum(xtabs(~id,data=data)/180)
## [1] 132
# show how many lists
round(xtabs(~list, data=data)/180)
## list
##  1  2  3  4  5  6 
## 25 20 23 15 27 22

Set up factors.

data$conditions <- droplevels(as.factor(data$conditions))
data$id <- droplevels(as.factor(data$id))
data$slider_value <- as.numeric(data$slider_value)
data$dist <- ifelse(data$list<4, "sharp", "blurred")
data$dist <- droplevels(as.factor(data$dist))

Deal with missing data

#coercion slider value when missing list to NA (due to unexpected technical errors).
missing_list <- which(is.na(data$list)==TRUE)
data[missing_list,]$slider_value <- NA

#coercion slider value when none fit to NA. This refers to the third option "neither".
data$slider_value <- ifelse(data$slider_value==-1, NA, data$slider_value)

Subset filler from main data set.

data_filler <- subset(data, str_sub(data$conditions, 1, 1) == "f")
data <- droplevels(subset(data, str_sub(data$conditions, 1, 1) != "f"))

Encode slider. The max (100) position is randomized on the slider, either Left or Right. For Left, we have 1 -100 to get the same result as Right, which is the default.

data$prefer_first_1st <- ifelse(data$leftright_trial=="1left", 100-data$slider_value, data$slider_value)
data$prefer_first_1st <- data$prefer_first_1st - 50 # We further extract 50 to get the range from -50 to 50 for better visualization.

Encode condition’s name into English.

data$combination <- as.factor(ifelse(str_sub(data$conditions, 3, 4)=="cf", "color_form",
                           ifelse(str_sub(data$conditions, 3, 4)=="dc", "dimension_color",
                                  "dimension_form")))


data$relevant_property <- as.factor(ifelse(str_sub(data$conditions, 1, 1)== "e", "first",
                                           ifelse(str_sub(data$conditions, 1, 1)== "z", "second",
                                           "both")))

Descriptive Statistics

Aggregate data.

#aggregate data
aggregate(data$prefer_first_1st, list(data$conditions), FUN = function(x){c(mean(x), sd(x)/sqrt(length(x)))})
##   Group.1 x.1 x.2
## 1    brcf  NA  NA
## 2    brdc  NA  NA
## 3    brdf  NA  NA
## 4    ercf  NA  NA
## 5    erdc  NA  NA
## 6    erdf  NA  NA
## 7    zrcf  NA  NA
## 8    zrdc  NA  NA
## 9    zrdf  NA  NA
aggregate(data$prefer_first_1st, list(data$conditions, data$dist), FUN = function(x){c(mean(x), sd(x)/sqrt(length(x)))})
##    Group.1 Group.2       x.1       x.2
## 1     brcf blurred        NA        NA
## 2     brdc blurred        NA        NA
## 3     brdf blurred        NA        NA
## 4     ercf blurred        NA        NA
## 5     erdc blurred        NA        NA
## 6     erdf blurred        NA        NA
## 7     zrcf blurred        NA        NA
## 8     zrdc blurred        NA        NA
## 9     zrdf blurred        NA        NA
## 10    brcf   sharp        NA        NA
## 11    brdc   sharp        NA        NA
## 12    brdf   sharp 23.923077  1.421155
## 13    ercf   sharp        NA        NA
## 14    erdc   sharp        NA        NA
## 15    erdf   sharp        NA        NA
## 16    zrcf   sharp -5.261866  1.709208
## 17    zrdc   sharp        NA        NA
## 18    zrdf   sharp 13.715221  1.639330
ratings_aggregated<-aggregate(prefer_first_1st~combination+relevant_property+dist, data=data, mean)
#TODO: find another way to compute ses (SEwithin?)
ses_ratings<-aggregate(prefer_first_1st~combination+relevant_property+dist, data=data, function(x) {sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))})
ratings_aggregated<-cbind(ratings_aggregated,ses_ratings[,4])

names(ratings_aggregated)[5]<-"se"

Draw a distribution plot of slider.

# Raw data
ggplot(data, aes(x = prefer_first_1st, fill = relevant_property)) +
  geom_density(fill = CSP_colors[1])

Draw a summary plot of slider by conditions.

# make bar plot for descriptive analysis
p <- ggplot(ratings_aggregated, aes(x=combination, y=prefer_first_1st, fill=relevant_property)) +
  ylab("preference for the position in front")+
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge()) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=prefer_first_1st-se, ymax=prefer_first_1st+se), width=.2,
                position=position_dodge(.9))+
  facet_wrap(~dist)+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Paired") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1))
p

ggsave(paste0(plots_dir, "/descriptive_plot.png"), plot = p, device = "png", width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)

Inferential Statistics

Normality test.

nortest::ad.test(data$prefer_first_1st) # Not passed with raw data. -> We need to transform the data.
## 
##  Anderson-Darling normality test
## 
## data:  data$prefer_first_1st
## A = 934.59, p-value < 2.2e-16

Data transformation.

data$sli<-(data$prefer_first_1st+50.5)/101
model_raw <- lmer(data$prefer_first_1st~relevant_property*combination*dist
           +(1|id)
           +(1|item), data=data)
model_log <- lmer(log(sli)~relevant_property*combination*dist
           +(1|id)
           +(1|item), data=data)
data$transli<-qlogis(jitter(data$sli))
model_logit <- lmer(transli~relevant_property*combination*dist
             +(1|id)
             +(1|item), data=data)
par(mfrow=c(1,3))
qqnorm(residuals(model_raw), main = "default value")
qqnorm(residuals(model_log), main = "value log transformed")
qqnorm(residuals(model_logit), main = "value logit transformed")

# We will use logit transformed data for further analysis.
model_full <- model_logit
step(model_full) # No sig. three way interaction. We can reduce model
## Backward reduced random-effect table:
## 
##            Eliminated npar logLik   AIC    LRT Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## <none>                  21 -27941 55925                         
## (1 | id)            0   20 -28356 56752 829.70  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## (1 | item)          0   20 -27947 55933  10.49  1   0.001202 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Backward reduced fixed-effect table:
## Degrees of freedom method: Satterthwaite 
## 
##                                    Eliminated  Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF
## relevant_property:combination:dist          1  71.865  17.966     4 10240
## relevant_property:combination               0 128.777  32.194     4 10244
## relevant_property:dist                      0 149.361  74.680     2 10244
## combination:dist                            0  92.613  46.307     2 10234
##                                    F value   Pr(>F)   
## relevant_property:combination:dist  1.4780 0.205894   
## relevant_property:combination       2.6481 0.031608 * 
## relevant_property:dist              6.1427 0.002157 **
## combination:dist                    3.8089 0.022205 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Model found:
## transli ~ relevant_property + combination + dist + (1 | id) + (1 | item) + relevant_property:combination + relevant_property:dist + combination:dist
model_full <- update(model_full, .~.-relevant_property:combination:dist)

We want to resolve the interaction between distribution and combination first.

# Subset the data to dimension_X and color_X
data_dimisionX <- subset(data, combination=="dimension_form" | combination=="dimension_color")
data_colorForm <- subset(data, combination=="color_form")

# Fit two models for each subset
model_dimisionX <- lmer(transli~relevant_property*dist
             +(1|id)
             +(1|item), data=data_dimisionX)

model_colorForm <- lmer(transli~relevant_property*dist
             +(1|id)
             +(1|item), data=data_colorForm)

# Check the interaction between relevant_property and dist
step(model_dimisionX) # Sig. Interaction between relevant_property and dist. -> Further Anylysis to explain it needed.
## Backward reduced random-effect table:
## 
##            Eliminated npar logLik   AIC  LRT Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## <none>                   9 -18038 36094                       
## (1 | item)          1    8 -18038 36092    0  1          1    
## (1 | id)            0    7 -18605 37224 1134  1     <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Backward reduced fixed-effect table:
## Degrees of freedom method: Satterthwaite 
## 
##                        Eliminated Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value   Pr(>F)
## relevant_property:dist          0 139.28  69.643     2 6759.8  6.6779 0.001267
##                          
## relevant_property:dist **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Model found:
## transli ~ relevant_property + dist + (1 | id) + relevant_property:dist
step(model_colorForm) # No sig. Interaction between relevant_property and dist. -> We can look at the summary now and make conclusions.
## Backward reduced random-effect table:
## 
##            Eliminated npar  logLik   AIC    LRT Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## <none>                   9 -9676.8 19372                         
## (1 | id)            0    8 -9725.0 19466 96.382  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## (1 | item)          0    8 -9695.1 19406 36.716  1  1.367e-09 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Backward reduced fixed-effect table:
## Degrees of freedom method: Satterthwaite 
## 
##                        Eliminated  Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value  Pr(>F)
## relevant_property:dist          1   58.58   29.29     2 3357.4  2.1395 0.11787
## relevant_property               0 2433.16 1216.58     2 3359.4 88.8001 < 2e-16
## dist                            0   57.32   57.32     1  130.4  4.1842 0.04281
##                           
## relevant_property:dist    
## relevant_property      ***
## dist                   *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Model found:
## transli ~ relevant_property + dist + (1 | id) + (1 | item)
summary(model_colorForm) 
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: transli ~ relevant_property * dist + (1 | id) + (1 | item)
##    Data: data_colorForm
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 19353.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.50022 -0.83809  0.06006  0.80986  2.12515 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  id       (Intercept)  0.9503  0.9749  
##  item     (Intercept)  0.3007  0.5484  
##  Residual             13.6910  3.7001  
## Number of obs: 3516, groups:  id, 132; item, 27
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error         df t value
## (Intercept)                         -0.15180    0.22523  192.01651  -0.674
## relevant_propertyfirst               1.50159    0.22059 3358.15997   6.807
## relevant_propertysecond             -0.78811    0.22078 3357.84101  -3.570
## distsharp                            0.65432    0.27624  366.20509   2.369
## relevant_propertyfirst:distsharp    -0.58255    0.30598 3357.56212  -1.904
## relevant_propertysecond:distsharp   -0.07691    0.30600 3357.32177  -0.251
##                                   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                       0.501128    
## relevant_propertyfirst            1.17e-11 ***
## relevant_propertysecond           0.000362 ***
## distsharp                         0.018372 *  
## relevant_propertyfirst:distsharp  0.057014 .  
## relevant_propertysecond:distsharp 0.801563    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                (Intr) rlvnt_prprtyf rlvnt_prprtys dstshr rlvnt_prprtyf:
## rlvnt_prprtyf  -0.490                                                  
## rlvnt_prprtys  -0.489  0.500                                           
## distsharp      -0.638  0.399         0.399                             
## rlvnt_prprtyf:  0.353 -0.721        -0.360        -0.554               
## rlvnt_prprtys:  0.353 -0.360        -0.721        -0.554  0.500
# 1. Sharp increase color first ordering in color form; 
# 2. Sharp 
summary(model_dimisionX)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: transli ~ relevant_property * dist + (1 | id) + (1 | item)
##    Data: data_dimisionX
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 36076.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3669 -0.3960  0.1286  0.6092  2.4870 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  id       (Intercept)  2.498   1.581   
##  item     (Intercept)  0.000   0.000   
##  Residual             10.429   3.229   
## Number of obs: 6894, groups:  id, 132; item, 27
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error         df t value
## (Intercept)                          1.31712    0.22059  172.52333   5.971
## relevant_propertyfirst               0.67462    0.14036 6761.10218   4.806
## relevant_propertysecond             -1.32501    0.13694 6759.38653  -9.676
## distsharp                            0.65094    0.30657  171.04023   2.123
## relevant_propertyfirst:distsharp    -0.05091    0.19205 6759.92314  -0.265
## relevant_propertysecond:distsharp    0.57492    0.18949 6758.97425   3.034
##                                   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                       1.31e-08 ***
## relevant_propertyfirst            1.57e-06 ***
## relevant_propertysecond            < 2e-16 ***
## distsharp                          0.03517 *  
## relevant_propertyfirst:distsharp   0.79096    
## relevant_propertysecond:distsharp  0.00242 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                (Intr) rlvnt_prprtyf rlvnt_prprtys dstshr rlvnt_prprtyf:
## rlvnt_prprtyf  -0.305                                                  
## rlvnt_prprtys  -0.313  0.492                                           
## distsharp      -0.720  0.220         0.225                             
## rlvnt_prprtyf:  0.223 -0.731        -0.360        -0.306               
## rlvnt_prprtys:  0.226 -0.356        -0.723        -0.311  0.496        
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

For Dimension X data, we further split the data by distribution.

emmeans(model_dimisionX, pairwise~relevant_property * dist)
## $emmeans
##  relevant_property dist      emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  both              blurred  1.31712 0.221 Inf     0.885     1.749
##  first             blurred  1.99174 0.222 Inf     1.556     2.428
##  second            blurred -0.00789 0.220 Inf    -0.440     0.424
##  both              sharp    1.96806 0.213 Inf     1.551     2.385
##  first             sharp    2.59178 0.213 Inf     2.174     3.009
##  second            sharp    1.21797 0.213 Inf     0.801     1.635
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast                       estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  both blurred - first blurred    -0.6746 0.140 Inf  -4.806  <.0001
##  both blurred - second blurred    1.3250 0.137 Inf   9.676  <.0001
##  both blurred - both sharp       -0.6509 0.307 Inf  -2.123  0.2751
##  both blurred - first sharp      -1.2747 0.307 Inf  -4.157  0.0005
##  both blurred - second sharp      0.0992 0.307 Inf   0.323  0.9995
##  first blurred - second blurred   1.9996 0.140 Inf  14.306  <.0001
##  first blurred - both sharp       0.0237 0.308 Inf   0.077  1.0000
##  first blurred - first sharp     -0.6000 0.308 Inf  -1.949  0.3723
##  first blurred - second sharp     0.7738 0.308 Inf   2.513  0.1201
##  second blurred - both sharp     -1.9759 0.306 Inf  -6.451  <.0001
##  second blurred - first sharp    -2.5997 0.306 Inf  -8.487  <.0001
##  second blurred - second sharp   -1.2259 0.306 Inf  -4.002  0.0009
##  both sharp - first sharp        -0.6237 0.131 Inf  -4.758  <.0001
##  both sharp - second sharp        0.7501 0.131 Inf   5.727  <.0001
##  first sharp - second sharp       1.3738 0.131 Inf  10.485  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 6 estimates
# first blurred - first sharp     -0.5979 0.308 Inf  -1.944  0.3754
emmeans(model_dimisionX, pairwise~dist)
## $emmeans
##  dist    emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  blurred   1.10 0.206 Inf     0.697      1.50
##  sharp     1.93 0.199 Inf     1.536      2.32
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: relevant_property 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  blurred - sharp   -0.826 0.286 Inf  -2.883  0.0039
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: relevant_property 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic
# blurred - sharp   -0.825 0.286 Inf  -2.885  0.0039
load("./data_preprocessed_huashan.RData")
model_full <- lmer(prefer_first_1st~relevant_property*combination*dist
             +(1|id)
             +(1|item), data=data)
step(model_full) # sig. interaction relevant:dist
## Backward reduced random-effect table:
## 
##            Eliminated npar logLik   AIC     LRT Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## <none>                  15 -47595 95221                          
## (1 | id)            0   14 -48136 96300 1080.93  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## (1 | item)          0   14 -47612 95252   33.23  1  8.178e-09 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Backward reduced fixed-effect table:
## Degrees of freedom method: Satterthwaite 
## 
##                                    Eliminated Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF
## relevant_property:combination:dist          1   4676    2338     2 9392.8
## relevant_property:combination               2   4584    2292     2 9394.8
## combination:dist                            3   2731    2731     1 9397.3
## combination                                 0 494150  494150     1 9401.3
## relevant_property:dist                      0  13708    6854     2 9398.5
##                                     F value    Pr(>F)    
## relevant_property:combination:dist   1.9289  0.145368    
## relevant_property:combination        1.8902  0.151094    
## combination:dist                     2.2521  0.133465    
## combination                        407.4309 < 2.2e-16 ***
## relevant_property:dist               5.6512  0.003525 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Model found:
## prefer_first_1st ~ relevant_property + combination + dist + (1 | id) + (1 | item) + relevant_property:dist
model_full <- update(model_full, .~.-relevant_property:combination:dist - relevant_property:combination - combination:dist)
step(model_full)
## Backward reduced random-effect table:
## 
##            Eliminated npar logLik   AIC     LRT Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## <none>                  10 -47609 95238                          
## (1 | id)            0    9 -48149 96316 1080.38  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## (1 | item)          0    9 -47625 95269   33.15  1  8.511e-09 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Backward reduced fixed-effect table:
## Degrees of freedom method: Satterthwaite 
## 
##                        Eliminated Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF  F value
## combination                     0 494150  494150     1 9401.3 407.4309
## relevant_property:dist          0  13708    6854     2 9398.5   5.6512
##                           Pr(>F)    
## combination            < 2.2e-16 ***
## relevant_property:dist  0.003525 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Model found:
## prefer_first_1st ~ relevant_property + combination + dist + (1 | id) + (1 | item) + relevant_property:dist
data_dimisionX <- subset(data, combination=="dimension_X")
data_colorForm <- subset(data, combination=="color_form")

model_dimensionX <- lmer(prefer_first_1st~relevant_property*dist
             +(1|id)
             +(1|item), data=data_dimisionX)

summary(model_dimensionX) # relevant_propertyfirst:distsharp     5.7283     1.9311 6171.7906   2.966  0.00302 **
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: prefer_first_1st ~ relevant_property * dist + (1 | id) + (1 |  
##     item)
##    Data: data_dimisionX
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 61673.2
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.5537 -0.4235  0.1087  0.6775  2.4113 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  id       (Intercept) 329.2    18.1431 
##  item     (Intercept)   0.1     0.3163 
##  Residual             968.7    31.1232 
## Number of obs: 6315, groups:  id, 120; item, 27
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                    Estimate Std. Error        df t value
## (Intercept)                         24.5280     2.4914  144.1971   9.845
## relevant_propertyfirst               6.2880     1.3660 6178.2769   4.603
## relevant_propertysecond            -17.1889     1.3287 6168.5149 -12.936
## distsharp                           -0.8785     3.5792  143.9615  -0.245
## relevant_propertyfirst:distsharp     5.7283     1.9311 6171.7906   2.966
## relevant_propertysecond:distsharp   -0.1915     1.9048 6166.4165  -0.101
##                                   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                        < 2e-16 ***
## relevant_propertyfirst            4.25e-06 ***
## relevant_propertysecond            < 2e-16 ***
## distsharp                          0.80647    
## relevant_propertyfirst:distsharp   0.00302 ** 
## relevant_propertysecond:distsharp  0.91993    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##                (Intr) rlvnt_prprtyf rlvnt_prprtys dstshr rlvnt_prprtyf:
## rlvnt_prprtyf  -0.262                                                  
## rlvnt_prprtys  -0.270  0.492
